profiles.title: Terminal / Palantir Mission-Control
profiles.register: Enterprise mission-control, operationally serious, cinematic but inspectable.
profiles.scene_sentence: A senior operator is standing in a dim industrial command room at dusk, watching a physical operation resolve into a precise AI control layer.
use_when: The buyer needs trust, precision, and operational seriousness.
The product lives in logistics, defense, infrastructure, manufacturing, security, AI operations, or industrial automation.
The landing page needs to feel premium and high-stakes without becoming fantasy sci-fi.
avoid_when: The brand needs warmth, friendliness, consumer play, or soft creator energy.
The product has no concrete operational proof or serious workflow to show.
The page would become illegible if it used dark cinematic density.

typography.voice: Large clean grotesk for claims, tight technical labels for overlays, numeric markers for sections.
rules: Use big type for category-level claims only.
Use compact labels for HUD, proof, and operational state.
Keep letter spacing neutral; avoid trendy negative tracking.
Keep product copy in HTML, not baked into generated media.

profiles.density: High signal, medium-to-high information density, low decoration.
motion_attitude: stateful overlays that feel like instrumentation
dos: Make the first viewport feel like a real operation.
Use overlays as evidence of system intelligence.
Let proof sections breathe after dense cinematic scenes.
donts: Do not use decorative blobs or random particle fields as the main visual.
Do not over-stack Spline, WebGL, frame sequences, and shader effects in the same beat.
Do not hide the product category until late scroll.
Do not let cinematic darkness reduce text contrast or mobile readability.

reference_examples.label: Terminal Industries
reference_examples.notes: Dark industrial command register, neon CTA, grid/HUD overlays, and proof-forward enterprise story.
